info.name: magiq-media API
info.description:
MAGIQ Media API — multi-tenant, event-sourced media asset management platform.

## Setup
1. Create a Postman environment using the variables below.
2. Set `BASE_URL`, `OWNER_TOKEN`, and `SYSTEM_TOKEN` for your target environment.
3. Run the **Auth / WhoAmI** request first to verify your token is valid.

## Environments
| Variable | Local | Dev |
|---|---|---|
| `BASE_URL` | `http://localhost:5006` | `https://api.dev.magiqmedia.com/v1` |
| `OWNER_TOKEN` | local dev JWT | long-lived test JWT |
| `SYSTEM_TOKEN` | local system JWT | long-lived system JWT |

## Conventions
- All mutating requests auto-generate a fresh `IdempotencyKey` header via the collection pre-request script.
- `TenantId` is never sent in the request body — it is sourced from the `tenant_id` JWT claim.
- All error responses use `Content-Type: application/problem+json` (RFC 9457).

variable.description: JWT with actor_type=User. Set per environment.
JWT with actor_type=System. Required for privileged endpoints.
Pre-existing MediaItem ID for upload-with-item scenarios.
Pre-signed S3 URL — set by upload initiation.
S3 multipart UploadId — set by initiate-multipart.
media-processing SQS queue URL. Local/dev only.

exec: // Auto-generate IdempotencyKey for every mutating request

request.description: Returns the email, tenant_id and subject extracted from the bearer JWT. Use to verify your token is valid before running scenarios.

item.name: AssetManagement
item.description:
All AssetManagement endpoints covering business scenarios A-1 through AM-7.

See spec/contexts/AssetManagement/business-scenarios.md for full domain flow detail.

item.name: A-1 — Single-Part Upload and Process
item.description:
Upload a media asset linked to an existing MediaItem. Steps: initiate → S3 PUT (manual) → confirm → poll.

Preconditions: MediaItem exists in Draft status; MEDIA_ITEM_ID variable set.

request.description: Issues a pre-signed S3 PUT URL. ASSET_ID is caller-generated (UUID v7 recommended; $guid used here for convenience). mediaItemId links the asset to an existing Draft MediaItem.

PUT the binary file directly to S3 using the pre-signed URL captured from step 1.

In local environments (LocalStack): attach test-image-small.jpg from tests/fixtures/.
This step does NOT go through the API — it talks directly to S3.

After a successful PUT, S3 fires an ObjectCreated notification → SQS → Ingest API calls /confirm automatically. In non-local environments step 3 (confirm) is not needed.

exec: // Capture ETag in case it is needed for multipart flows

request.description:
Manually advances the asset from Pending → Validating, bypassing the S3 event trigger.

Use in test/local environments only. In production, this is called internally by the Ingest API on receipt of the S3 ObjectCreated SQS notification.

exec: // Negative: replay same IdempotencyKey — expect cached 202

request.description:
Poll until status = Active. Processing pipeline: Validating → Processing → Active.
Recommended: max 12 retries, 5-second interval (60s budget).

exec: pm.test('All returned assets belong to the requested MediaItem', () => {
pm.test('All returned assets have status Active', () => {
pm.test('Pagination shape correct (nextPageToken may be null)', () => {

request.description: Expects 401 — no Authorization header.
Expects 404 — mediaItemId does not exist.
Expects 409 — asset is already past Pending (Active after A-1 completed).

exec: pm.test('409 Conflict — asset not in Pending', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));

item.name: A-2 — Standalone Upload (No MediaItem)
item.description:
Upload an asset without a MediaItem (drag-and-drop). The asset processes through the full pipeline. Assign to a MediaItem role afterwards.

Preconditions: No MediaItem required for upload. MEDIA_ITEM_ID must be set for the assign step.

request.description: mediaItemId is omitted — standalone (drag-and-drop) upload. Full processing pipeline runs regardless of MediaProfile capabilities.
Attaches the standalone asset to a MediaItem role. Asset.MediaItemId is set permanently.

item.name: A-3 — Processing Failure Recovery
item.description:
Verifies the failure paths: worker error and saga timeout.

Preconditions: ASSET_ID points to an asset in Processing or ProcessingFailed status. SAGA_ID set for the force-expire path.

request.description: Poll after a known processing failure. Expects status = ProcessingFailed.
Non-production only. Immediately triggers the timeout handler for a saga in ProcessingDispatched state, simulating a SagaTimeoutScanner expiry. Requires SYSTEM_TOKEN and SAGA_ID.

exec: // After expiry, poll the asset — expect ProcessingFailed

item.name: 3. Poll Asset After Timeout — Verify ProcessingFailed

exec: pm.test('Status is ProcessingFailed after saga timeout', () => {
pm.test('Asset is NOT Active (no retry occurred)', () => {

request.description: Expects 409 — saga already in terminal state (Complete or Failed).

exec: pm.test('409 Conflict — saga already terminal', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));

item.name: AM-4 — Multipart Upload (Large File)
item.description:
Upload a large asset (> 50 MB) via S3 multipart upload. Steps: initiate → PUT parts to S3 → complete. Abort path also covered.

Preconditions: MEDIA_ITEM_ID set (optional).

request.description: Starts an S3 multipart upload. Part count = ceil(contentLength / partSize). Default partSize = 50 MB → 2 parts for 100 MB.

exec: pm.test('Each part has partNumber, uploadUrl, expiresAt', () => {
pm.test('Part numbers are sequential starting at 1', () => {
// Store part 1 URL for documentation — real parts are PUT client-side

request.description:
PUT part 1 directly to S3 using the pre-signed URL from parts[0].uploadUrl.
Capture the ETag response header — required for the complete request.

Repeat for each part using the corresponding uploadUrl.
Signals all S3 parts have been uploaded. The server calls S3.CompleteMultipartUpload. Asset transitions Pending → Validating. ETags must exactly match values returned by S3.

Aborts the in-progress multipart upload. Releases S3 part storage. Asset transitions to MultipartAborted (terminal).

Run this INSTEAD of step 3 to test the abort path. Requires a fresh ASSET_ID in Pending/Multipart state.
Expects 422 — S3 rejects CompleteMultipartUpload due to ETag mismatch.

exec: pm.test('422 Unprocessable Entity — ETag mismatch', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));

request.description: Expects 409 — asset is already Active or in a non-multipart-pending state.

exec: pm.test('409 Conflict — not pending multipart', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));

item.name: AM-5 — Virus Scan Failure
item.description:
System-driven scenario. No user API call triggers the failure — poll to observe the terminal state.

Setup: upload test-infected.eicar via the upload-url flow in a non-production environment. The Processing Worker runs the virus scan and transitions the asset to InfectionDetected.

item.name: 1. Initiate Upload with EICAR Test File

request.description: Use the EICAR test string file (68 bytes). The virus scanner will detect the signature and transition the asset to InfectionDetected.
Poll until status = InfectionDetected. This is a terminal state — no retry is attempted.

exec: pm.test('Asset cannot be re-processed (InfectionDetected is terminal)', () => {
// Informational: no renditions should be present
// renditions should be empty for an infected asset

request.description: Expects 409 — InfectionDetected is not an archiveable status (only Active and ProcessingFailed are).

item.name: AM-6 — Archive Asset
item.description:
Owner-initiated soft-delete. Asset must be Active or ProcessingFailed. S3 objects are retained.

Preconditions: ASSET_ID points to an Active asset (run A-1 first).

request.description: Soft-deletes the asset. Dispatches ArchiveAssetCommand. S3 objects retained. Integration event media.asset.archived emitted to Billing and Notifications.
Expects 409 — asset is already Archived.
Uses SYSTEM_TOKEN with a different owner identity. Expects 403 — caller does not own this asset.

exec: pm.test('403 Forbidden — not asset owner', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Run against an asset in Validating/Processing state. Expects 409 with currentStatus != Active.

exec: pm.test('409 Conflict — status not archiveable', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));
pm.test('currentStatus is not Active or ProcessingFailed', () => {

item.name: AM-7 — Hard Delete
item.description:
Permanently deletes an Archived asset. S3 objects (original + all renditions) are deleted synchronously before AssetDeleted is emitted.

Preconditions: ASSET_ID points to an Archived asset (run AM-6 first).

request.description: Permanently deletes the asset and all S3 objects. S3 deletion is synchronous — event is only emitted after S3 confirms. Billing quota released on success.
After hard delete, the read model record is removed. Expects 404.

exec: pm.test('404 Not Found — asset deleted', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

request.description: Run against an Active asset (not archived). Expects 409 with errorCode=AssetNotArchived. Use AM-6 first, then try to delete before archiving.

exec: pm.test('409 Conflict — asset not archived', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));

request.description: Expects 403 — caller does not own this asset.

exec: pm.test('403 Forbidden — not asset owner', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: ⚠️ DESTRUCTIVE — purges ALL data for the authenticated tenant. Requires SYSTEM_TOKEN (actor_type=System). Available only when ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Test.
